behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: Real failure: the automation analyzes unchanged lines or entire files instead of just diffs. An Integration test can catch this by mocking the diff source but using realistic diff structures with multiple files and changed/unchanged sections. Unit tests of individual functions might pass while the integrated diff-parsing logic fails. System/Real GitHub tests would be nice-to-have but not necessary if the diff parsing logic is tested with representative diff data.
behaviors.test_description: Create a multi-file diff with: (1) file A with 3 changed lines and 10 unchanged lines, (2) file B entirely unchanged, (3) file C with changed lines at various line numbers. Pass this realistic diff (or mock a GitHub API that returns it) to the automation's analysis pipeline. Assert that only the identified changed lines are passed to the agent for analysis, and no unchanged lines are included.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: No critical missed failures at Integration level. Diff parsing logic failure would be caught. (System tests with real GitHub diffs would add robustness for GitHub-specific edge cases, but not required for core behavior.)

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Real failure: comments posted to wrong line numbers, invalid commit SHAs, or nonexistent paths. A mock GitHub API can validate request parameters against documented API constraints (valid line ranges relative to the diff, required file paths, commit SHA format). Unit tests won't catch line-number calculation errors; only integration with a realistic diff structure will. System tests with real GitHub would catch unexpected API quirks, but Integration catches the core logic failure.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the GitHub API's comment posting endpoint. Run the automation on a realistic diff with intentional issues at known line numbers (e.g., issue on line 15, another on line 42). Intercept and validate every comment POST request: verify commit SHA is valid, file path exists in the diff, and line number is within the changed lines or nearby context. Assert all comments were posted with correct parameters.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: No critical missed failures. Line-number calculation errors would be caught by the mock validation. (Real GitHub tests might catch unexpected API behavior, but documented constraints are sufficient for this level.)

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Real failure: the agent fails to detect introduced bugs. Mocking the agent defeats the purpose—we'd always pass by hardcoding a response. Only a real LLM call on real or realistic code with seeded bugs tests actual detection capability. Integration tests with synthetic agent responses cannot validate true defect-finding ability.
behaviors.test_description: Create one or more test PRs with intentionally introduced bugs (e.g., missing error handling, off-by-one loop condition, unchecked null reference). Run the real agent on each PR. Verify that the agent identifies at least one real defect related to the introduced bug. (Acceptance: identifies the bug in at least one test case, tolerating minor variations in how the bug is described.)
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: No missed failures. Real agent is required. (Tests may be flaky due to LLM non-determinism, so multiple runs or clear bug patterns reduce flakiness.)

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to an inline comment that was also posted—no findings appear in the summary without a matching inline comment.
behaviors.justification: Real failure: inconsistency between inline comments and summary (e.g., 5 issues identified but only 3 in summary, or summary mentions issues not in inline comments). This is deterministic logic that can be tested by controlling the agent's output and GitHub's mock responses. No real LLM or GitHub API required; the bug is in the summary-building or comment-posting orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the agent to return N deterministic findings (e.g., 5 findings with specific descriptions). Mock GitHub API to track all posted inline comments. Run the full automation flow. Collect all inline comments and the final summary comment. Verify: (1) exactly N inline comments were posted, (2) the summary mentions all N findings, (3) no findings appear in the summary without a matching inline comment, (4) no inline comments exist without being mentioned in the summary.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: No critical missed failures at Integration level. Logic is deterministic. (System/Workflow tests would verify this works with real agents/APIs, but are not needed for this specific consistency behavior.)

behaviors.description: The full sequence—reading the diff, posting inline comments, posting the summary comment—completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Real failure: the full pipeline fails end-to-end (crashes, hangs, incomplete output, or incorrect behavior with real code and real systems). This requires real GitHub API interaction (System level: network, API endpoints, rate limits) and real agent analysis (Agentic level: LLM non-determinism, realistic findings). Mocking both would hide real integration failures. Full Workflow level with real PR, real agent, and real GitHub API is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test pull request on GitHub (or test instance) with realistic code changes and seeded issues. Run the full end-to-end automation: read the diff from the real PR, analyze with the real agent, post inline comments to the real PR, post the summary comment. Verify: (1) process completes without crashing or timeout, (2) inline comments appear at the correct lines in the GitHub PR, (3) summary comment is posted and references all findings, (4) findings in the summary are substantive and match the code issues.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: No critical missed failures at Workflow level. This is the full end-to-end test. (Tests may be slow or occasionally flaky due to LLM non-determinism, but that's inherent to the behavior being tested.)
